title: Introduction
description: Introduction.

title: Story about Annie Mariah Healey; relationship with Native Americans; Joseph War; foodstuffs; early Genesee
description: "Turkey In The Straw" played on the harmonium. Recitation of printed story about Annie Mariah Healey, born October 1071 in Thorn Creek, first white girl born in county. Relationship with Indians. Thorn Creek P.O. Joseph War. Foodstuffs. Early Genesee.

title: Story about Dan Healey; fencing rails; railroad in Genesee
description: Recitation of story about Dan Healey. Going to mountains for fence rails. 1886 railroad arrives in Genesee and Genesee moves.

title: Story about Tom Tierney; how Thorn Creek gets its name; fixing up the homestead
description: Story about Tom Tierney. Thorn Creek gets its name. Tierney builds 'hide-out' from Indians. 1882, daughter Annie goes to DeSmet Mission. Fixing up the home stead.

title: Camas; breaking sod with walking plow; horse-trading; Pony Express rider; Tierneys move to Moscow
description: Thorn Creek ran year-around. Camas. Breaking sod with walking plow. To town once a year for winter supplies. Horse-trading with Indians. Pony Express rider in Colorado. Tierneys move to Moscow in 1901.

title: Grass hay for beds; Grand Central Hotel; Genesee businesses; stagecoach
description: Dan Healey, an Irishman. Grass hay for beds. S5.50 per week at Grand Central Hotel. Early Genesee businesses. Stagecoach.

title: Tierneys leave Denmark
description: Tierneys leave Denmark.

title: John Tierney
description: Newspaper account of John Tierney.

title: Tierney's youngest son dies
description: Tierney's youngest son dies on Bataan march.

title: Helped organize St. Boniface parish; Cataldo and DeSmit helped build the community
description: Fr. Cataldo used to stop over at Tierney's. Helped organize St. Boniface parish. Mrs. Jones' mother went to DeSmet mission at urging of Fr. Cataldo. Henry Spaulding brother-in-law to Felix Warren. Cataldo and DeSmet contributed to building-up of community.

title: Native Americans during the war; healing burns with coyote tallow
description: Keeping door locked because of Indians. Indian atrocities during war. Indians fishing on the Snake. Healing burns with coyote tallow.

title: Mother married early; father had leg amputated; selling hogs; 1893 depression; curing hams
description: Mother married before she was 15. Father breaks leg and has to have it amputated. Hogs go down to 1 and a half cents a pound. Hard work and hogs keep him from going under during 1893 depression. Curing hams. Baumberg's fine bologna.

title: Harvesting wheat; first thrashing machines
description: Harvesting wheat with scythe and cradle. First thrashing machines known as "coffee mills."

title: Wild Davey worked for Tierneys; sandpoint banker
description: "Wild Davey" used to work for Tierney's. Wild Davey and the Sandpoint banker.

title: Pet pigs; raising geese
description: Pet pigs. Raising geese.

title: Early Genesee; small farms; prophecy about airplanes
description: Early Genesee, small farms. Uncle's prophecy about airplanes, degeneration of farm soil.

title: Latah County pioneers
description: Newspaper story about Latah County pioneers

title: Introduction
description: Introduction.

title: Cooking for thrashing crew
description: Cooking for thrashing crew.

title: Men on thrashing crews; selling chickens and eggs; trading
description: Characters of the men on the thrashing crews. Selling dressed chickens and eggs; trading for merchandise.

title: Relationship with Native Americans; huckleberry picking; Genesee churches; childhood
description: Relationship with Indians. Huckleberry picking. Churches in Genesee. Childhood in Genesee.

title: Tierney's settling in Genesee; early socializing
description: Tierney's settled near Genesee because country was so open. Early socializing.

title: Hauling timber from mountains
description: Hauling timber from the mountains for settler's cabins.

title: 1893 wet harvest
description: 1893 Wet harvest. Turning hogs into the wheat stacks to recoup the loss.

title: Fights between men
description: Fights. Men carried blackjacks.

title: Growing watermelons; Tierney's Pony Express career
description: Growing watermelons for sale. Felix warren, stagecoach driver. Tierney's Pony Express career lasted six months.

title: Gamble family
description: Gamble Family.

title: Changes in the country
description: Changes in the country.

title: Preserving foods
description: Preserving food. Root cellars. Canning berries. Foods.

title: Hired labor; pet horse
description: Building built by hired labor. Hired men and hired women. Pet horse.

title: "Annie Laurie" on the melodian
description: "Annie Laurie" on the melodian.
